mechanism_narrative: A persistent positive month-over-month trend in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate earnings capacity, which is incorporated gradually into equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 3-month trend and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month span, it signals an emerging labor market deterioration that precedes broader earnings downgrades, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as markets price in a slowdown.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 across triggered subsample.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of low FEDFUNDS relative to rising CPIAUCSL (negative real policy rate) signals accommodative real financial conditions that incentivize risk-asset allocation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; the effect requires both series moving in this combined configuration and is not present from either series alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12m CPIAUCSL inflation rate < 0
failure_condition: Rank IC of the combined real-rate signal versus forward 20d returns is not statistically distinguishable from the single-series FEDFUNDS-only model (p > 0.10) over a 3-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS rising over prior 6 months), a decline in HOUST signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand that foreshadows a broader consumption slowdown, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; in easing or flat-rate regimes the same HOUST decline carries no reliable directional signal.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% over trailing 3 months while FEDFUNDS has risen over trailing 6 months
failure_condition: Negative HOUST signal fails to produce below-median forward 60d returns in tightening regimes across more than half of historical tightening episodes since 1990.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in PAYEMS growth signals strengthening consumer income and corporate hiring confidence, which markets reward with short-term positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as payroll data surprises are incorporated into equity risk premia immediately following release.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly change and forward 5-day SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or reaction is symmetric regardless of PAYEMS sign.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS (retail sales) above its 6-month trailing average signals robust consumer demand supporting corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, an effect strongest when unemployment is low and stable.
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns following RSAFS trigger are not significantly different from unconditional baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly GDPC1 growth decelerates below its trailing 4-quarter average while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, markets interpret this as a stalled recovery unable to be offset by monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; the effect depends on the joint condition of weak growth and constrained policy space.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < trailing 4-quarter average while FEDFUNDS < 1%
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive median forward 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, contradicting the hypothesized negative mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS held near historic lows (below 1%) for an extended period reflects sustained accommodative policy that compresses equity risk premia and supports valuation multiples, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as long as the low-rate regime persists.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day SPX returns during FEDFUNDS<1% periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05) across the walk-forward evaluation.
